title: trifolium angustifolium subsp. gibellianum
intro: Trifolium angustifolium subsp. gibellianum, also known as the narrow-leaved red clover, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing menstrual cramps, alleviating symptoms of menopause, and soothing digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Trifolium angustifolium subsp. gibellianum include infusions, tinctures, and dried flowers, which can be used to make tea, capsules, or topical creams.
